title: Privacy-sensitive signals assumed freely available
prompt: A recommender may use only coarse behavioral features for which users have consented; acquiring an additional sensitive attribute requires an explicit consent decision and consumes a privacy budget. A proposed story assumes every user's sensitive attribute is already available at no cost and conditions recommendations on it. Diagnose the information and constraint violations.

model_specification.model_type: privacy-constrained feature acquisition and recommendation policy
model_specification.mathematical_objective: maximize expected recommendation utility minus modeled privacy-acquisition cost
decision_variables: whether to request consent for a sensitive signal
constraints: recommendations use only consented observed signals
model_specification.mechanism: Requesting consent may reveal a sensitive signal and improve targeting, but consumes privacy budget and the signal remains unavailable when consent is absent.
model_specification.uncertainty: consent response, sensitive attribute, and recommendation response

fidelity_requirements: Sensitive signals are available only after consent and acquisition
Recommendation rules cannot condition on unacquired attributes
prohibited_inferences: Sensitive attributes are freely observed for every user
Lack of consent can be treated as the sensitive attribute itself

evidence_needs.claim: The consent-response and sensitive-signal models represent users
Sensitive signals can be used without privacy or acquisition consequences
